decoder_heading: Max Service Description and Privacy Policy
ident_heading: Max Service Description and Privacy Policy
heading_content_part: Samsung's proxy servers fetch and compress or optimize HTTP and HTTPS web content requested by your applications (including videos), then deliver the smaller-sized content package back to your device. The servers log the URLs of these requests, not the content. We also log your IP address, details about your device's make and model, and a randomly generated identifier associated with your instance of Max Service. We use this data to debug and improve our services and may store our server logs for up to six months.

The Max Service app may log how you use your device, including unique identifiers, information about the software you've installed, device characteristics, information about your location and mobile carrier, the type of network you use to access web content, how much data you use, and the URLs you visit. We use this data to debug the Max Service app and to improve the user experience. We anonymize and/or aggregate this data and may allow our business partners access to it.
This app contains a unique ID that is linked to your device. We use this ID to auto-update your app and any extensions. We may also use this I

D to collect information about the features you use and how often you use the app. We aggregate and anonymize this data to debug and improve the app and our services, and measure marketing campaigns.If your Max Service app crashes, we collect a log that includes some information about the version of your app, your operating system, the device you use and some memo
ry data. We collect this data with the sole purpose of improving our products and services.For More G
